forecast-period.text: A high pressure system centred just south of Tasmania will move southeastwards during Sunday. This will allow a trough over WA to link to a weak front south of the Bight then move over western and southern parts of SA later Sunday into Monday. Another high pressure system is expected to follow early next week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Areas of high cloud with isolated showers about the southwest, extending to reach eastern parts of the agricultural area during the evening. Possible thunderstorms about the southwest and about western and central southern coastal parts. Mostly sunny in the far north. Maximum temperatures above average. Southeast to easterly winds in the northeast. Northeasterly winds otherwise, ahead of a southerly change in the southwest during the evening.

High cloud and isolated showers about the southern agricultural area, mainly about eastern parts clearing to the east in the morning. Possible showers to follow about southern coasts and the Lower South East. Mostly sunny in the north. Maximum temperatures above to well above average. North to northwesterly winds ahead of a southwest to southerly change in the west and south, extending inland during the afternoon and evening.

Light showers about the southern agricultural area, mainly about windward coasts and ranges during the morning. Middle level cloud, isolated showers and possible thunderstorms about the southwest, extending across the west during the day, possibly reaching central parts in the evening. Mostly sunny in the north. Maximum temperatures near average to slightly below average about the agricultural area, grading to above average in the north. Northeast to northerly winds in the north at first, with southwest to southeasterly winds extending throughout during the day.

text.p: Light showers about the southern agricultural area, mainly about southern coasts and ranges during the morning. Middle level cloud, isolated showers and possible thunderstorms about the southwest, extending across the south of the Pastorals and parts of the northern agricultural area during the morning, before contracting eastwards and clearing in the evening. Maximum temperatures near average to slightly below average about the southeast, grading to above average in the north and west. Southeast to northeasterly winds tending north to northwesterly in the northwest.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Wednesday: Generally less than 3mm about the agricultural area and south of the pastoral districts, increasing to 2 to 10 mm about the southwest and about southern coasts and ranges. isolated falls of 10 to 30 mm possible with thunderstorms.
